articles.title: Arizona files landmark criminal charges against prediction market Kalshi
articles.summary: Arizona prosecutors hit Kalshi with criminal charges, marking the first time a state has brought criminal action against a major prediction market platform. This sets a dangerous precedent that could trigger a domino effect of state-level crackdowns just as prediction markets were gaining mainstream acceptance.

articles.title: $1.2M bet on whether there's federal charges in Minneapolis shootings
articles.summary: Someone placed a $1.2M wager on whether federal charges will be filed in connection with recent Minneapolis shootings. The massive bet size signals either insider confidence or serious money chasing what could be predictable legal outcomes based on federal prosecution patterns.

articles.title: Major prediction market faces 34th country ban
articles.summary: Another country has banned a major prediction market platform, bringing the total number of jurisdictions blocking access to 34. The growing regulatory crackdown signals that governments view prediction markets as gambling rather than information aggregation tools, forcing platforms to choose between compliance costs and global reach.

articles.title: U.S. Democratic lawmakers introduce bill to crack down on prediction markets
articles.summary: Democratic lawmakers want to restrict prediction markets, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket that have exploded in popularity during election season. If it gains traction, this could force major platforms to delist political markets or face regulatory crackdowns, potentially pushing the action back to offshore sites where U.S. bettors already wager billions.

articles.title: A journalist reported a missile strike. Then came the death threats.
articles.summary: A journalist faced death threats after reporting on a missile strike, highlighting the dangerous intersection of war reporting and online harassment. Information warfare and journalist safety directly impact the reliability of conflict reporting that prediction markets depend on for geopolitical bets.

articles.title: Make the Predictions Come True
articles.summary: Some prediction markets are shifting from pure forecasting to actively trying to influence the outcomes they're betting on. This blurs the line between predicting and manipulating events, which could either supercharge market accuracy or completely undermine their credibility as information sources.

articles.title: Perfection is nearly impossible: Kalshi is giving away $1 billion for a perfect bracket
articles.summary: Kalshi is offering $1 billion to anyone who can correctly predict every game in March Madness, joining the long tradition of impossible bracket challenges that generate massive publicity while costing almost nothing. The stunt shows how prediction markets are using lottery-style promotions to attract mainstream attention, even though the mathematical odds make Warren Buffett's old $1 billion bracket bet look generous.

articles.title: Gamblers trying to win a bet on Polymarket are vowing to kill me if I don’t rewrite an Iran missile story
articles.summary: Polymarket bettors are apparently threatening violence against a journalist over how they covered Iran's missile attacks, presumably because the story's framing could affect bet outcomes. This shows how high-stakes prediction markets can turn users into deranged partisans who view news coverage as market manipulation rather than reporting.

articles.title: The Polymarket Gamblers Betting on the Value of Your Apartment
articles.summary: Polymarket traders are now placing bets on real estate prices, turning your local housing market into another prediction market playground. This signals prediction markets are expanding beyond politics and sports into everyday economic indicators that actually affect people's wallets, creating new opportunities for informed bettors to profit from housing knowledge.

articles.title: Wall Street Is Already Betting on Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Major financial institutions are placing bets that prediction markets will become a massive asset class, likely through direct investments or by launching their own platforms. This institutional validation could pump billions into the space and legitimize betting on everything from elections to Fed policy decisions.

articles.title: Traders Flip Senate Control Bet as Democrats Overtake Republicans on Kalshi, Polymarket
articles.summary: Prediction market traders are now betting Democrats will control the Senate after the 2024 elections, with the party's odds surging past Republicans on both Kalshi and Polymarket. This flip signals either new polling data, campaign developments, or smart money spotting value that the broader market missed.

articles.title: Polymarket Five-Minute Bets Capture ‘Addictive’ Crypto Craze
articles.summary: Polymarket launched five-minute betting windows that let traders make rapid-fire predictions on crypto price movements, tapping into the same dopamine-driven mechanics that make slot machines irresistible. The move signals prediction markets are abandoning their wonky policy-forecasting roots to chase the lucrative world of high-frequency gambling addiction.
